content:
I could not find any verified medical literature or clinical guidelines referencing an "Okonkwo-Prasad criteria" or an "NI (neural inflammation) quotient" calculated from preoperative CRP and IL-6 levels specifically for postoperative delirium risk stratification, nor a protocol involving prophylactic dexmedetomidine for hip fracture patients over 75 under that name.

It is possible that this is a very recent, unpublished, or institution-specific protocol, or a hypothetical scenario. If you have a source or more context, please share it, and I can help interpret or summarize it.

As of current established clinical practice and guidelines, postoperative delirium risk stratification typically involves clinical risk factors (age, cognitive impairment, comorbidities) rather than a calculated neural inflammation quotient from CRP and IL-6. Prophylactic dexmedetomidine use is sometimes considered in high-risk
